acceptance_criteria: The post explicitly explains why public securities have different functional roles across entity types.
External constraint and imported-essential dependence are visibly named as real limits.
The post acknowledges omitted collateral/liquidity and holder-composition layers without sounding evasive.
The new prose clarifies rather than bloats the argument.
constraints: Do not imply a full bond-market or repo model exists if it does not.
Keep the tone explanatory and humble, not defensive.
Do not let the new material dominate the post's main arc.
description: Add a scoped explanation that bonds are not just 'borrowing' in the household sense; explain why securities can serve different functions for issuers, currency users, and quasi-sovereigns; add concise treatment of external constraint and imported essentials; and add clear limitations language for collateral/repo, holder composition, and maturity structure where they are not yet fully modeled.
goal: Revise the post so bonds, securities, external constraint, and omitted financial-plumbing layers are acknowledged in the right places without diluting the main claim.
implementation_notes: Completed the prose refinement in content/posts/money-minsky-policy-mismatch.md. Added a 'Bonds are not one thing' subsection, clarified that treasuries and central banks care about reserves, settlement, collateral quality, maturity structure, and liquidity conditions, and named rollover pressure, holder composition, collateral/liquidity context, and external dependence as constraint qualifiers rather than ignored factors.
title: Prose pass: bonds, liability hierarchy, and scoped omissions
